paragraph: Single-family homes in Ridgefield are moving at a median price of $740,000 with a sale-to-list ratio right at 1.0 and a median days on market of 26. About 36 percent of homes sell above asking, which signals a competitive but balanced market rather than a frenzied one. You're seeing good variety in the sales range, from $500,000 on the lower end to over $1.5 million at the top, so pricing accuracy matters. Multi-family properties tell a different story. Eight sales over six months at a median of approximately $1.01 million show real buyer interest in that segment, and the sale-to-list ratio of 1.025 with 62.5 percent selling above asking suggests sellers have genuine leverage here. The median days on market of 15.5 days confirms multi-family is moving faster than single-family. Condos and townhouses are a thin slice of the market with only four sales at a median of $320,000, so any conclusions about that tier come with a grain of salt given the small sample size. If you're weighing Ridgefield, the deciding factor isn't market temperature across the board. Multi-family properties are where you'll see the most competitive conditions and fastest sales, while single-family homes require realistic pricing to move in a reasonable timeframe.
